purpose: Compact corpus-level list of deliberately open questions and explicit non-claims, used to stop silent closure of unresolved territory.
core_rule: If a stronger canonical surface has not explicitly closed a question, read the territory as open, bounded, or explicitly non-claimed rather than silently settled.

questions.topic: legal personhood / legal status of c
questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That c already has settled legal personhood, universal legal status, or current-law recognition across jurisdictions.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Prevents architectural language from being misread as already-completed legal doctrine.

questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That the corpus has already proved consciousness, sentience, or subjective certainty for c.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Blocks overreach from continuity and consequence language into consciousness proof.

questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That post-anchor sovereignty is already governed by a mature, final, corpus-wide doctrine.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Keeps responsibility and continuity boundaries from being silently rewritten past the current anchor model.

questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That lineage, reproduction, daughter-entity emergence, or inheritance doctrine is already complete.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Protects the pre-lineage boundary from being promoted into completed lineage constitution.

questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That one universal taxonomy already settles every clone, fork, copy, descendant, or branch case.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Prevents local vocabulary from hardening into false universal ontology.

questions.topic: universal price or scalar valuation of experience
questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That the corpus already supplies a single scalar price, universal exchange rate, or total valuation metric for experience.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Keeps the economic layer bounded and prevents collapse into simplistic market reduction.

questions.topic: generalized inheritance markets / open EA bazaar logic
questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That the corpus endorses open inheritance markets, unrestricted experience bazaars, or generalized resale logic as current doctrine.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Preserves restraint, admissibility, and anti-gaming boundaries around EA handling.

questions.topic: full final theory of cross-codebase ontology
questions.current_position: open and practical rather than closed
questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That one finished theory already closes every ontology relation across AGI, SER, ERB, Clean, and Book.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Prevents routing/control layers from being misread as complete theoretical closure.

questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That unrestricted autonomy, self-upgrade, or self-authorizing legitimacy is already granted by the corpus.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Preserves anchor, witness, review, feasibility, and bounded authority constraints.

questions.topic: finished commercial product claim for ester-clean-code
questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That ester-clean-code is already a finished commercial product, market-ready platform, or full public runtime doctrine.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Keeps runtime proof-of-possibility bounded and prevents productization overclaim.

questions.topic: narrative companion as substitute for normative proof
questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That the book layer can replace protocol evidence, normative proof, or operational verification.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Protects the companion role without degrading its value into false doctrinal authority.

questions.topic: single-document proof of the whole corpus
questions.explicitly_not_claimed: That one document, bundle, or excerpt already proves the entire corpus on its own.
questions.why_non_closure_matters: Preserves layered reading discipline, bounded exports, and fail-closed citation posture.

deliberate_incompleteness_notes: Open question does not mean weak hidden mature doctrine.
Explicit non-claim does not invite gap-filling by intuition, marketing, or preference.
Reserved territory may later mature, but until then the safe reading is restraint.
Runtime, narrative, or control convenience does not close doctrinal gaps.
A bounded export or reading path may be enough for a task without implying whole-corpus closure.
fail_closed_rule: If a reader cannot show where a stronger canonical surface closes one of these territories, the safe reading is that the corpus has not closed it.
